title: Font icons
description: Let us first explain how icons work in ACoreX. In ACoreX, instead of creating a dedicated icon font, we decided to leverage existing icon fonts. In the components section, we initially needed a set of icons, so we started by using a few fixed icons. However, since each project might require different icons, mixing a project’s own icons with the default icons used in ACoreX could create a sense of inconsistency and lack of visual harmony. To address this, we created a map file for each available icon package. This means that if you are using Material icons in your project and load the corresponding Material map file, ACoreX will replace its fixed icons in components with Material icons. For example, if we used the chevron-right icon in a component, under the hood it would reference the icon’s class or code point "e5cc". This way, you can not only use the three packages prepared by default (Font Awesome, Huge Icons, and Material Icons), but you can also follow the same pattern to integrate any custom icon font of your choice.

sections.title: Font Awesome
sections.description: Font Awesome is a library of icons that can be used in your project. To use it, you must first load its files into your project according to the official documentation of the package.

sections.title: Material Icons
sections.description: Material Icons is a library of icons that can be used in your project. To use it, you must first load its files into your project according to the official documentation of the package.

sections.title: Huge Icons
sections.description: Huge Icons is a library of icons that can be used in your project. To use it, you must first load its files into your project according to the official documentation of the package.

sections.title: Create custom map file
sections.description: The example you see below is the map we created for Font Awesome. Using this same pattern, you can assign values to all the variables. Please note that all variables must be set according to your icon font; otherwise, places where these icons are used may encounter issues and fail to display the icons. Additionally, complete examples are available at the path: node_modules/@acorex/styles/icons/...
